purpose: Define configurable external login entry buttons/options per tenant, independent from internal module entitlements.
clarification: This contract is not about internal module visibility after login. It is specifically about which login entry options appear on the external login screen: admin, shopper, client portal, representative, coordinator, consultant/franchisee, etc.
rules: External login entry options must be configurable per tenant.
Hiding a login entry option is a UX/business configuration and must not be treated as the only security layer.
A tenant may enable regional/local access models without limiting the platform globally.
Client portal login can be hidden initially or shown as preview sales depending on tenant configuration.
Shopper login can be hidden for tenants that do not operate shopper recruitment directly.
Admin/ops/coordinator/representative entries must map to Auth claims and permissions later, but this contract does not activate Auth real.
No login option should be hard-coded for all tenants.

tyaInitialRecommendation.notes: Paula may choose to show only admin and shopper initially for TyA, and optionally client portal as commercial preview. This is external login visibility, not internal module capability.

newLessons: Difference between login entry visibility and internal module permissions
How to configure which roles appear on the login screen
How to use client portal as commercial preview

hardStops: Do not hard-code login entry options globally.
Do not confuse external login entry visibility with internal module entitlements.
Do not treat hidden login option as backend security.
Do not activate Auth real from this contract.
Do not write Firestore from this contract.
